sourceNotes.purpose: Explicit no-hotspot visit intent for validator clarity. This supplements the main case matrices and records the chosen progression rule for each flagged visit.
sourceNotes.currentRosterPolicy: Route-only verification visits in the live roster auto-complete on entry. Interrogation-driven capstone visits stay no-hotspot and progress through suspect nodes and authored outcomes.
sourceNotes.pilotUpgradePolicy: Some auto-complete visits remain candidates for later suspect-driven upgrades in the showcase pilot pass.

visits.reason: This stop is a route-verification beat, not a clue hunt. Entering it should count as completing the travel-distance proof.
visits.futureUpgradeNote: Can remain a compact route-check even if Patrol later gains optional suspect pressure.

visits.reason: The visit exists to spatially confirm the impossible station-to-warehouse timing, so it should clear immediately on arrival.
This is an off-site payoff visit for the broken gallery alibi rather than a second evidence room.
The shipped case still uses the alley as a physical handoff confirmation stop, so entry should count as clearing the route beat.
visits.futureUpgradeNote: Showcase pilot intent still upgrades this visit into an Owen Kade interrogation stop once the deeper suspect flow is wired.

visits.reason: The second visit is there to visualize the pre-emptive warehouse purge, not to introduce a new discovery board.
This is a suspect-pressure stop. The elevator visit is intentionally no-hotspot because Owen's answer, not a click target, completes the progression beat.
In the current shipped roster the marina stop is a clean route confirmation visit. It should complete as soon as the player reaches the dockside path.
visits.futureUpgradeNote: Showcase pilot intent can later promote this visit into a dockside witness pressure beat.

visits.reason: This overlook visit is a spatial confirmation of the false-search route and does not need its own hotspot interaction.
The berth visit is a destination proof beat after the enhanced cargo tag reveal, so arrival should satisfy the visit.
The service-ramp visit exists to physically verify Vera reached the annex, so it should clear on entry instead of waiting for a nonexistent hotspot.
The corridor is an interrogation setup stop. Gideon's answer is the progression hook that opens the transfer-rack leg.
The final rack visit is meant to resolve through Inez's last pressure beat, not through a hotspot click.
